name: Mango (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight, ripe pulp (Tommy Atkins / Keitt / Kent / Haden cultivars)

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Principal free sugar of ripe mango pulp; sucrose > fructose > glucose.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Least-abundant of the three free sugars.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Reported 0 g in ripe pulp; retained from prior profile but effectively absent.

components.name: starch
components.evidence: High (up to ~15%) in green fruit; hydrolyses to sugars during ripening, leaving low residual starch in ripe pulp. Value is representative/ripeness-dependent.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid of mango; range 0.13-0.71 g/100g fresh wt across cultivars/ripeness, midpoint used. WAS MISSING from prior profile.

components.name: L-malate
components.source: PMC10808196 (137 mg/100g naturally ripened); Medlicott & Thompson 1985 doi:10.1002/jsfa.2740360707
components.evidence: Second major organic acid; declines modestly during ripening.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Minor organic acid detected in ripening mango; not reliably quantified (trace).

components.name: tartaric acid
components.evidence: Trace organic acid; present but unquantified.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: Trace organic acid; present but unquantified.

components.name: quinic acid
components.evidence: Reported among mango non-volatile acids; not quantified.

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Low total protein; amino acids below are protein-derived profile.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid (0.158 g/165g serving).

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Not resolved separately from methionine in USDA data; present in trace.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Very low-fat matrix; fatty acids below are minor.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (C18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Main polyunsaturated fatty acid in mango pulp.

components.name: palmitic acid (C16:0)
components.evidence: Principal saturated fatty acid (total SFA 0.092 g/100g).

components.name: dodecanoic acid (C12:0, lauric)
components.evidence: Listed for mango but only trace; retained without a reliable quantity.

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Range 13-93 mg/100g across cultivars; USDA mean 36.4.

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: 54 ug RAE (equivalent to 1082 IU, which is often mislabeled as ug elsewhere). From provitamin-A carotenoids; no preformed retinol.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Fe (single consolidated entry; prior profile split it into Fe2+/Fe3+/compartment duplicates).

components.name: gallic acid
components.evidence: Genuine mango phenolic acid; retained from prior profile, not reliably quantified in fresh pulp.

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: Synthetic pharmaceutical estrogen (contraceptive); biologically implausible in mango; FooDB predicted/erroneous entry.

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Bacterial polymer used as a food preservative; not a native mango metabolite; FooDB predicted/additive.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae/Rutaceae (parsnip, citrus), not mango; FooDB predicted/implausible.

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: Aroma volatile listed as trace/predicted; not a growth-relevant nutrient of the mango matrix.

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Non-physiological stereoisomer artifact; L-isoleucine already covered.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive ultratrace/contaminant element, not a defined mango growth nutrient.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt ion at ultratrace/contaminant level; not a quantified mango nutrient.

remove.name: Iodine c
remove.reason: Iodine is negligible/not characteristic in mango; trace-contaminant noise.

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: Molybdenum ultratrace; not a quantified, growth-defining mango nutrient.

remove.name: Boron
remove.reason: Ultratrace element; not a growth-relevant mango nutrient (kept out of components).

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Redundant compartmentalized duplicate of iron; consolidated into single 'iron' entry.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Redundant oxidation-state duplicate of iron; consolidated into single 'iron' entry.

remove.name: Carbonate
remove.reason: Environmental/model exchange species, not a food-composition nutrient.

missing_added: citric acid (DOMINANT organic acid, absent from prior profile)
succinic / tartaric / oxalic / quinic acids (minor organic acids)
quantitative amounts for all sugars (sucrose 6.97, fructose 4.68, glucose 2.01 g/100g)
quantitative vitamins, minerals, carotenoids and fatty acids
keep_uncovered: mangiferin (defining mango xanthonoid; no BiGG metabolite)
pelargonidin (genuine mango phenolics/flavonoids/anthocyanins, no BiGG metabolite)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169910 (Mangos, raw; SR Legacy)
Maldonado-Celis et al. 2019, Front. Plant Sci. doi:10.3389/fpls.2019.01073 (Chemical Composition of Mango Fruit)
Medlicott & Thompson 1985, J. Sci. Food Agric. doi:10.1002/jsfa.2740360707 (sugars & organic acids in ripening Keitt mango, HPLC)
PMC10808196 (physiological/biochemical variation of naturally ripened mango; citric 376, malic 137 mg/100g)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a growth medium are the free sugars sucrose (~7 g/100g) > fructose (~4.7) > glucose (~2), plus citric acid (~0.3 g/100g) as the principal organic acid with malate (~0.14) secondary. Citric acid was the key growth-relevant omission from the prior FooDB profile. Values are ripe-pulp means; organic-acid and vitamin-C levels vary strongly with cultivar and ripeness (citric 0.13-0.71 g/100g). Removed a synthetic estrogen, a preservative polymer, an Apiaceae furanocoumarin, stereoisomer/compartment duplicates, and ultratrace contaminant elements as FooDB noise.
